examples.title: OS Service Request Example
examples.description: Demonstrates how application programs request OS services through system calls
examples.code:
// System Call Interface for OS Services
PROCEDURE RequestOSService(serviceType, parameters)
BEGIN
 SWITCH serviceType
 CASE FILE_OPEN:
 RETURN SystemCall(OPEN_FILE, parameters)
 CASE MEMORY_ALLOCATE:
 RETURN SystemCall(ALLOCATE_MEM, parameters)
 CASE PROCESS_CREATE:
 RETURN SystemCall(CREATE_PROCESS, parameters)
 CASE READ_INPUT:
 RETURN SystemCall(READ_DEVICE, parameters)
 CASE COMMUNICATE:
 RETURN SystemCall(SEND_MESSAGE, parameters)
 END SWITCH
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Applications use system calls to request OS services. Each service type corresponds to a specific system call.

examples.title: Resource Allocation Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates OS resource allocation among competing processes
examples.code:
// Simplified Resource Allocation Algorithm
CONSTANTS
 MAX_PROCESSES = 10
 RESOURCES = [CPU, MEMORY, DISK, PRINTER]

DATA STRUCTURE
 ProcessTable: Array of process control blocks
 ResourceTable: Array of resource allocation status

PROCEDURE AllocateResources(processID, requiredResources)
BEGIN
 FOR each resource IN requiredResources
 IF ResourceTable[resource].available > 0 THEN
 ResourceTable[resource].available--
 ResourceTable[resource].allocatedTo[processID] = TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FAIL // Resource not available
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN SUCCESS
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE ReleaseResources(processID)
BEGIN
 FOR each resource IN RESOURCES
 IF ResourceTable[resource].allocatedTo[processID] = TRUE THEN
 ResourceTable[resource].available++
 ResourceTable[resource].allocatedTo[processID] = FALSE
 END IF
 END FOR
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Shows basic resource allocation mechanism where OS tracks available resources and allocates them to processes.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of resources
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is number of processes

examples.title: Error Detection Handler
examples.description: Pseudocode for OS error detection and handling mechanism
examples.code:
// Error Detection and Handling
PROCEDURE InitializeErrorHandling()
BEGIN
 RegisterInterruptHandler(HARDWARE_ERROR, HandleHardwareError)
 RegisterExceptionHandler(SOFTWARE_ERROR, HandleSoftwareError)
 RegisterTrapHandler(MEMORY_ERROR, HandleMemoryError)
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE HandleHardwareError(errorCode)
BEGIN
 LogError("Hardware Error detected:", errorCode)
 IF errorCode IS RECOVERABLE THEN
 AttemptRecovery()
 ELSE
 NotifyUser(errorCode)
 TerminateProcess(currentProcess)
 END IF
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE HandleMemoryError(address, errorType)
BEGIN
 IF errorType = PAGE_FAULT THEN
 HandlePageFault(address)
 ELSE IF errorType = SEGMENTATION_FAULT THEN
 TerminateProcess(currentProcess)
 RaiseSignal(PROCESS_TERMINATED)
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how OS detects and handles various errors through interrupt and exception handlers.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for detection, O(n) for recovery

examples.title: Process Communication
examples.description: Demonstrates inter-process communication using shared memory
examples.code:
// Inter-Process Communication (IPC) - Shared Memory
// Process A (Writer)
PROCEDURE WriteToSharedMemory(data)
BEGIN
 sharedMemPtr = AttachSharedMemory(SHARED_SEGMENT_ID)
 IF sharedMemPtr != NULL THEN
 CopyDataToMemory(sharedMemPtr, data)
 DetachSharedMemory(sharedMemPtr)
 SignalSemaphore(DATA_READY)
 END IF
END PROCEDURE

// Process B (Reader)
PROCEDURE ReadFromSharedMemory()
BEGIN
 WaitSemaphore(DATA_READY)
 sharedMemPtr = AttachSharedMemory(SHARED_SEGMENT_ID)
 IF sharedMemPtr != NULL THEN
 data = CopyDataFromMemory(sharedMemPtr)
 DetachSharedMemory(sharedMemPtr)
 RETURN data
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Shows two methods of IPC - shared memory and semaphores for synchronization between processes.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is data size
